---
title: "Deep Research Example: Algonquin Land Claim"
subtitle: "Gemini Deep Research on the 1835--1836 Algonquin negotiations"
---

## About This Example

The following report was generated by **Gemini Deep Research** (Google) in 2026, in response to a query about the Algonquin land claim negotiations concerning L'Isle-aux-Allumettes in the Ottawa Valley, 1835--1839.

The output is reproduced here with formatting cleaned up but no content altered. Unlike the [marine insurance example](deep-research-example.qmd), where verification revealed systematic fabrication, this report surfaced genuinely important sources that the researcher had not encountered in three years of conventional archival work. However, not every detail in the report is necessarily accurate --- some narrative elements may be embellished or inferred beyond what the sources support. See our [Deep Research Tools](deep-research.qmd) page for the full comparison of these two cases and a verification workflow.

::: {.callout-note}
## This Report Contains Both Genuine Discoveries and Potential Errors

The key sources identified in this report --- particularly Brian Lawson's 2009 conference paper and the Government of Canada publication --- were verified and proved to be real and relevant. However, other details (specific meeting dates, narrative embellishments, characterizations of individuals' motivations) have not been independently verified against primary sources. This is the point of the example: even when a deep research report makes a genuine contribution, it must still be read critically. See our [Deep Research Tools](deep-research.qmd) page for discussion.
:::

---

{{< include "Algonquin Land Claim Investigation.md" >}}
